results.summary: All file paths, commands, service details, and configuration references verified against codebase. Docker images (postgres:17.9, keycloak:26.2.3, nginx:1.25-alpine), container names, compose files, and directory structure all match.

issues.claim: Cloudflare Tunnel config references 'class.noda.co.nz' as a hostname routed through nginx
issues.actual: config/cloudflare/config.yml has no 'class.noda.co.nz' entry. Only 'noda.co.nz', 'auth.noda.co.nz', 'localhost.noda.co.nz', 'health.noda.co.nz' exist. The class.noda.co.nz domain resolves via Cloudflare but is not explicitly listed in the tunnel config.

issues.claim: docker/services/postgres/init/ directory exists for initialization scripts
issues.actual: docker/services/postgres/init/ does not exist. Only docker/services/postgres/init-dev/ exists. The base docker-compose.yml mounts ./services/postgres/init but that directory under docker/services/ does not exist. The init scripts are at services/postgres/init/ (top-level services/).

issues.claim: Backup directories docker/services/postgres/backup/ and docker/volumes/backup/ are production backup paths
issues.actual: docker/services/postgres/backup/ does not exist. docker/volumes/backup/ does exist and is the active backup volume mount. The prod compose also references ./services/postgres/backup which does not exist under docker/services/.

issues.claim: docker/services/ contains keycloak themes
issues.actual: docker/services/keycloak/ directory does not exist. Keycloak configuration is at services/keycloak/ (top-level). The docker-compose files mount ./services/keycloak/themes but that themes subdirectory also does not exist under services/keycloak/.

issues.claim: config/environments/.env.production exists as a file
issues.actual: config/environments/.env.production does not exist. Only .env.production at the project root exists. The config/environments/ directory only contains .env.example and .env.production.template.

issues.claim: KEYCLOAK_DB_PASSWORD is defined in .env.example
issues.actual: The .env.example file defines KC_DB_PASSWORD (not KEYCLOAK_DB_PASSWORD). However, Docker Compose actually uses POSTGRES_PASSWORD as KC_DB_PASSWORD, which is correctly noted in the description.

issues.claim: docker/services/postgres/ only has init-dev/ subdirectory, and conf/, init/, backup/ are not yet created
issues.actual: Partially correct. docker/services/postgres/ only has init-dev/. However, services/postgres/ (top-level) does have conf/ and init/ directories. The docker-compose.yml base file mounts ./services/postgres/init which resolves to docker/services/postgres/init (does not exist), not services/postgres/init.

results.summary: All file paths, commands, and environment variable references verified. Port mappings, service names, and deployment scripts all match the codebase.

All file paths, script references, configuration details, and port mappings verified. The deploy script step count (5 steps) matches the actual script. Shell script conventions, Dockerfile patterns, and backup system details all confirmed.
All 12 test scripts in scripts/backup/tests/ verified as existing. Dockerfile.test-verify confirmed as postgres:15-alpine based. TEST_REPORT.md exists. Library files in scripts/backup/lib/ all match.

issues.claim: init-databases.sh creates keycloak, keycloak_db, findclass_db, oneteam_prod, noda_prod databases
issues.actual: scripts/init-databases.sh only creates 2 databases: 'noda_prod' and 'keycloak'. The databases keycloak_db, findclass_db, and oneteam_prod are NOT in the REQUIRED_DBS array.

issues.claim: docker-compose -f docker/docker-compose.yml up -d postgres keycloak nginx
issues.actual: The correct command is 'docker compose' (space, not hyphen). The codebase uses Docker Compose V2 plugin throughout.

issues.claim: Container name noda-infra-postgres-1
issues.actual: The actual container name is noda-infra-postgres-prod (defined in docker-compose.yml line 15).

issues.claim: Container name noda-infra-nginx-1
issues.actual: The actual container name is noda-infra-nginx (defined in docker-compose.yml line 37). No '-1' suffix.

issues.claim: Container name noda-infra-postgres-1 for database check
issues.actual: The actual container name is noda-infra-postgres-prod. Using the wrong name would cause the command to fail.

issues.claim: docker-compose -f docker/docker-compose.yml restart postgres keycloak
issues.actual: Should be 'docker compose' (space, not hyphen). Also the dev compose overlay uses docker-compose but the standard is docker compose.

issues.claim: Data volume check doc at /scripts/backup/DATA_VOLUME_CHECK.md
issues.actual: The file exists at scripts/backup/DATA_VOLUME_CHECK.md (without leading /). The leading / makes it look like an absolute path, which would be incorrect.

issues.claim: init-databases.sh creates keycloak, keycloak_db, findclass_db, oneteam_prod, noda_prod databases
issues.actual: scripts/init-databases.sh only creates 2 databases: 'noda_prod' and 'keycloak'. The databases keycloak_db, findclass_db, and oneteam_prod are NOT in the REQUIRED_DBS array.

issues.claim: docker-compose -f docker/docker-compose.yml up -d postgres keycloak nginx
issues.actual: Should be 'docker compose' (space, not hyphen) for Docker Compose V2.

issues.claim: setup-keycloak-full.sh security check at line 54
issues.actual: The container running check is at line 41, not line 54. Line 54 reads the KEYCLOAK_ADMIN_PASSWORD environment variable.

issues.claim: init-databases.sh checks and creates keycloak_db, findclass_db, oneteam_prod, noda_prod
issues.actual: The actual script only creates 'noda_prod' and 'keycloak'. The other three databases (keycloak_db, findclass_db, oneteam_prod) are not in the REQUIRED_DBS array.

issues.claim: Container name noda-infra-postgres-1 in troubleshooting
issues.actual: The actual container name is noda-infra-postgres-prod.

results.summary: File paths verified. config/secrets.sops.yaml, config/keys/git-age-key.txt, config/secrets.local.yaml all exist. Scripts referenced (deploy-infrastructure-prod.sh, sops commands) are valid.

issues.claim: docs/ directory only contains README.md, DEPLOYMENT_GUIDE.md, architecture.md, KEYCLOAK_SCRIPTS.md, secrets-management.md
issues.actual: The actual docs/ directory also contains CONFIGURATION.md, GETTING-STARTED.md, DEVELOPMENT.md, and TESTING.md. The document structure listing is incomplete.

issues.claim: Container name noda-infra-postgres-1
issues.actual: The actual container name is noda-infra-postgres-prod.
